name: Humanitec Internal Developer Platform API
what_it_does: Internal Developer Platform (IDP) API — enabling platform engineering teams to build self-service infrastructure provisioning for developers via a Score workload specification, resource graph management, and deployment pipelines, with a REST API for managing applications, environments, workloads, and resource dependencies across Kubernetes clusters and cloud infrastructure.
use_cases: Building self-service deployment workflows via Humanitec API for developer platform automation
Managing environment and workload configurations via Humanitec API for multi-cluster Kubernetes
Implementing Score workload specs via Humanitec API for platform-agnostic app definitions
Automating environment provisioning via Humanitec API for staging/preview environment workflows
Integrating Humanitec with CI/CD via API for automated deployment pipeline triggers
not_for: Teams without Kubernetes infrastructure (Humanitec is Kubernetes-native)
Simple service catalogs without deployment automation (use Cortex or Port for catalog-only)
Small teams without platform engineering investment
best_when: A platform engineering team wants to implement an IDP with self-service Kubernetes deployment and infrastructure provisioning — particularly teams adopting Score for workload abstraction.
avoid_when: You're not using Kubernetes, want a catalog-focused IDP, or need a managed SaaS without self-hosting complexity.

auth.notes: API token via Authorization: Bearer header. Token from Humanitec account settings. Organization ID required in all API paths. REST API at api.humanitec.com. No OAuth2. Webhooks for deployment events. Score CLI for local workload management. humctl CLI for API interaction.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: up to 5 users, limited resources
pricing.notes: German company (Berlin). Founded 2019 by Kaspar von Grünberg. €24M+ funding. Platform engineering pioneers — published 'Platform Engineering Metrics' report. Score: open-source workload specification (CNCF Sandbox project). humctl: CLI for Humanitec management. Dynamic Configuration Management (DCM): manages environment-specific resource configs. Resource Graph: DAG of infrastructure dependencies. Competing with Port, Cortex, Backstage in IDP space. Strong Kubernetes/cloud-native community engagement. Annual Platform Engineering Report.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer token. Organization ID in all paths. Score CLI (open source). humctl CLI. GDPR. German company. Free plan (5 users). Webhooks. Kubernetes-native.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Application reads idempotent by ID. Environment reads idempotent. Deployment reads idempotent by ID. Resource graph reads idempotent. Most operations are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Organization ID required in all API paths: /orgs/{org_id}/apps/{app_id}/...
Score spec (score.yaml) defines workload independently of platform — key abstraction
Dynamic Configuration Management (DCM): environment-specific configs injected at deploy time
Resource Graph: dependencies (database, cache, queue) declared in Score, resolved by platform
humctl CLI wraps the REST API — use CLI for quick operations, API for automation
German company — GDPR by default; EU data residency preferred

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token. GDPR compliant (German company). SOC2 certified. Platform infrastructure data (deployments, configs, secrets references) is operationally sensitive. EU/US data residency. Score is CNCF Sandbox — community-audited. Dynamic Configuration Management handles secret injection — verify secret management integration.
